### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://massgrave.dev/windows_ltsc_links](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmassgrave.dev%2Fwindows_ltsc_links) ### Comment > Хочу сообщить о ложном срабатывании фильтра безопасности для домена buzzheavier.com. Ваш сервис блокирует его, помечая как вредоносный сайт. > > Этот ресурс используется проектом Microsoft Activation Scripts (MAS) для хранения оригинальных ISO-образов Windows. Файлы, размещенные там, проходят проверку и соответствуют официальным хэш-суммам MVS/OEM. Это легитимный ресурс для пользователей проекта, и блокировка препятствует доступу к проверенным образам системы. > > Прошу провести повторную проверку данного домена. issue.title: cwtv.com
issue.body:
### Prerequisites - [x] This site DOES NOT contain sexually explicit material, otherwise use [NSFW-specific form](./new?template=bug_report_NSFW.yml); - [x] Filters were updated before reproducing an issue; - [x] AdGuard product version is up-to-date; - [x] Browser version is up-to-date; - [x] If the site or app is broken, disabling AdGuard protection resolves an issue. ### What type of problem have you encountered? Website or app doesn't work properly ### Where is the problem encountered? https://cwtv.com ### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard DNS DNS setup: | Other DNS server: | Private System version: | `macOS 27` Browser: | Firefox Blocklists: | **General**
AdGuard DNS filter
AdGuard DNS Popup Hosts filter
AWAvenue Ads Rule
OISD Blocklist Small
**Other**
Dandelion Sprout's Game Console Adblock List
HaGeZi's Apple Tracker Blocklist
HaGeZi's Samsung Tracker Blocklist
Perflyst and Dandelion Sprout's Smart-TV Blocklist
HaGeZi's Windows/Office Tracker Blocklist
**Security**
Phishing URL Blocklist (PhishTank and OpenPhish)
Dandelion Sprout's Anti-Malware List
HaGeZi's Badware Hoster Blocklist
uBlock₀ filters – Badware risks
NoCoin Filter List
Scam Blocklist by DurableNapkin ### Add your comment and screenshots 1. Comment When using the "Protection against typosquatting" setting, the valid website for the US TV network The CW is blocked as it thinks it's typosquatting "cctv.com". Even adding it as a user rule doesn't fix it, the only way it works is by disabling the typosquatting setting entirely or turning off AdGuard DNS. Also I tried submitting the issue at https://reports.adguard.com/en/new_issue.html and it told me "captcha: This value is too long. It should have 2000 characters or less." right as I hit submit. 2. Screenshots
Screenshot 1:

issue.title: claude.ai

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://caude.ai/login?from=logout](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcaude.ai%2Flogin%3Ffrom%3Dlogout) ### Comment > URL: https://claude.ai/login?from=logout > > Claude is now using a clever tracking metric `?from=logout` > > If a user is found account-switching after burning their usage limits of their previous account, then their newly logged account would have less usage limits applied. > But, the problem is, this even applies for general account switching even if the usage limits are not hit on the first account! They track this constantly with `https://claude.ai/login?from=logout`. > > The `from=logout` specifically. This metric also BLOCKS opening the Google auto-signin dialog box (or the "auto-filler" in some cases in Android/ios, which makes it inherently harder for the user to sign-in to their account- as they now have to sign in by manually entering their username/email/pass. It's an annoyance/tracker. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/6faugk7z2skk0484k400wk48kgwcww0hrxqbxzmg20.png?nc=1)

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://caude.ai/login?from=logout](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcaude.ai%2Flogin%3Ffrom%3Dlogout) ### Comment > URL: https://claude.ai/login?from=logout > > Claude is now using a clever tracking metric `?from=logout` > > If a user is found account-switching after burning their usage limits of their previous account, then their newly logged account would have less usage limits applied. > But, the problem is, this even applies for general account switching even if the usage limits are not hit on the first account! They track this constantly with `https://claude.ai/login?from=logout`. > > The `from=logout` specifically. This metric also BLOCKS opening the Google auto-signin dialog box (or the "auto-filler" in some cases in Android/ios, which makes it inherently harder for the user to sign-in to their account- as they now have to sign in by manually entering their username/email/pass. It's an annoyance/tracker. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/6faugk7z2skk0484k400wk48kgwcww0hrxqbxzmg20.png?nc=1)

issue.title: This problem has resurfaced; can it be fixed again?

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

issue.body:
> [@jo2dan94](https://github.com/jo2dan94) Previously, this black screen delay could be completely removed, but after the update, it can no longer be eliminated. This black screen delay is actually a video ad on the website; the main video only loads after the ad has finished playing. Hopefully, this black screen delay can be resolved as soon as possible so that the main video plays directly after opening the app.
> 
> good news. it was just fixed by adam. he is a filter maintainer for adguard who does very good work. can you manually add these to your filters for now. the update should be avabile in a couple hours so just add these for now:
> 
> huavod.top,huavod.net,huavod.com,huaren.live#%#//scriptlet('set-constant', 'ConFig.ad_duration_ms', '0')
> huavod.top,huavod.net,huavod.com,huaren.live#%#//scriptlet('trusted-set-constant', 'mac_player_info.from', 'ARTVIP') 

 _Originally posted by @jo2dan94 in [#229498](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/229498#issuecomment-4275732146)_
